lint.command: echo "Skipping clippy due to known pre-existing issues in other workspace crates"

blockingIssues.description: `orient_parameter_loop()` still normalizes open section polylines with a signed-area comparison against the face boundary. That heuristic is only meaningful for closed loops; for plane-plane sections the sign depends on arbitrary sample ordering, so projected UV polylines can still be reversed inconsistently between faces. The fix commit adds `orient_open_section_pair()`, but it leaves this area-based normalization in place and even marks `section_projection_respects_inverted_face_orientation` ignored as a known limitation, so the original requirement that projection respects face orientation is still unmet.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align `rust-bop-worker` validator guidance with the mission config by replacing mandatory `cargo clippy -p truck-bop` with the repo-approved lint command from `.factory/services.yaml`, or explicitly document when clippy is intentionally skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `split-face-records` re-review still found `.factory/skills/rust-bop-worker/SKILL.md` requiring clippy while `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/28558208-113c-4faf-9654-e1aaded1d9ab/AGENTS.md` and `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/services.yaml` document that lint is intentionally skipped due to known failures outside `truck-bop`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify `rust-bop-worker` test-running guidance so workers do not treat mission feature IDs as exact Rust test names; recommend grep/discovery or pattern-based runs before using `-- --exact`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both the original `trimming-loops` review and the `fix-section-projection-storage` handoff show `cargo test -p truck-bop <feature-or-module-name> -- --exact` can succeed with zero matched tests, weakening verification.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the trimming-loop reversal invariant in `.factory/library/truck-bop.md`.
rejectedObservations.reason: Accurate but not yet clearly systemic; the rule is now encoded in code and tests, but we do not yet have repeated evidence that workers broadly miss it.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document split-face fragment ownership and hole containment invariants in `.factory/library/architecture.md`.
rejectedObservations.reason: Now accurate after the fixes, but still better treated as orchestrator-owned guidance so it can be integrated with the broader face-splitting design notes deliberately.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a convention update for FF open-curve orientation to shared docs right now.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because the implementation still has a known blocking limitation here; documenting it as settled guidance would overstate current behavior.
